findings.description: The PDF has the letterhead with ROY COOPER/Governor, MICHAEL S. REGAN/Secretary, BRIAN WRENN/Director positioned to the left with the state seal centered and NORTH CAROLINA/Environmental Quality below it, forming a traditional letterhead layout. The HTML renders these as a simple top-to-bottom list with the seal image below, losing the side-by-side letterhead structure.
findings.evidence: PDF: Officials listed on left side, seal centered at top. HTML: All elements stacked vertically in a single column.

findings.description: The signature image present in the PDF is missing from the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: PDF: Handwritten signature appears between 'Sincerely,' and 'David Miller, PE'. HTML: No signature image is present.

findings.description: The HTML adds a prominent heading 'Administrative Correction: Mine Permit #01-10' at the top that does not exist in the PDF document.
findings.evidence: PDF: No such heading exists; the document begins with the letterhead. HTML: Large heading 'Administrative Correction: Mine Permit #01-10' is displayed at the top of the page.

findings.description: Fabricated document title added as h1 heading that does not appear in the source PDF
findings.evidence:
The HTML contains

Administrative Correction: Mine Permit #01-10

which is not present anywhere in the source PDF. The PDF is a letter without a title heading. While an AI-generated title as h1 is noted as acceptable in the calibration guidelines, this is flagged for awareness as it could be confused with an official document title.

findings.description: The h1 heading text is fabricated - the PDF has no such title or heading structure
findings.evidence: The PDF is a standard business letter with no headings or sections. The HTML introduces 'Administrative Correction: Mine Permit #01-10' as an h1, which misrepresents the document structure by suggesting it has a formal title when it is simply a letter.
